description:
# Delete the per-callsite tuple-shape spec discovery code

## Goal

After the reducer has dissolved the calls that previously forced
per-tuple-shape spec minting, the discovery walk in
`ir_typer::walk_spec_for_discovery` is operating on a much smaller
input. The code paths that registered narrow specs per distinct
input-Descr tuple are now dead.

Delete them. Goldens from RED.6 must remain byte-identical.

## Specifics to remove

In `src/ir_typer.rs`:
- The closure_lit propagation path in `walk_spec_for_discovery` that
  pushes per-(captures + args) keys to `pending`.
- The `callsite_fn_consts` merge logic for inputs that don't survive
  reduction.
- Any helpers exclusively consumed by the above.

(Exact lines are best identified during implementation; the test is
'all goldens unchanged'.)

## Acceptance

- All goldens byte-identical to RED.6's blessed state.
- Cargo test green.
- LOC reduction: hundreds of lines from `ir_typer.rs`.

## Removals

This **is** the removal ticket.

comments.text: Empirical finding (attempted removal in this session): removing the closure_lit branch in walk_spec_for_discovery (src/ir_typer.rs:904-934) breaks RUNTIME tests for closure_typed_captures and curried_add (jit + aot paths) — the reducer dissolves the top-level CallClosure but the SURVIVING closure-body specs depend on per-(captures+args) narrow registration from the typer. The branch is therefore NOT dead. Safe removal requires either: (a) the reducer absorbing the narrow-spec discovery (e.g., when a CallClosure can't reduce, the reducer registers a spec request) or (b) confirming that closure_typed_captures' lowered IR doesn't need narrow specs (likely false). Reverted; ticket remains open.

title: fz-RED.8a REMOVE: per-callsite tuple-shape spec minting in walk_spec_for_discovery
